columns.comment: Computed PERSISTED column stripping protocol prefix and query parameters from ToAddress. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionOutputs)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing primary key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces)

columns.name: SentTransactionId
columns.comment: The sent transaction being replaced. FK to Wallet.SentTransactions.Id. Stays constant across replacements. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces)

columns.name: OldBlockchainTransactionId
columns.comment: The blockchain hash of the transaction being replaced (the stuck one). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces)

columns.name: NewBlockchainTransactionId
columns.comment: The blockchain hash of the replacement transaction (with higher fee). This becomes the new active hash. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp of the replacement event. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing primary key. FK target for Wallet.SentTransactionStatuses, Wallet.SentTransactionOutputs, and Wallet.SentTransactionReplaces. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: BlockchainTransactionId
columns.comment: The on-chain transaction hash/ID. Unique constraint enforced. Can be looked up on blockchain explorers. Format varies by blockchain (hex for ETH/BTC, base58 for SOL/XRP). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: The source wallet this transaction was sent from. FK to Wallet.Wallets.WalletId. For customer withdrawals, this is the customer's wallet. For redemptions, this is the system's omnibus/redeem wallet. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp when the transaction was broadcast to the blockchain. NULL only for legacy records. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.comment: Links to the parent request in Wallet.Requests.CorrelationId. Enables tracing from business request to on-chain transaction. NULL for pre-correlation-era transactions. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: TransactionTypeId
columns.comment: Business purpose: 0=Redeem, 1=CustomerMoneyOut, 2=AmlMoneyBack, 4=Funding, 5=ConversionMoneyIn, 6=ConversionMoneyOut, 7=Payment, 9=Staking, 10=BlockChainActivation, 11=OmnibusMoneyOut, 12=ConversionToFiat, 13=ManualUserMoneyOut, 14=StakeAndRewardsRefund, 15=CustomerMoneyBack. See Transaction Type. FK to Dictionary.TransactionTypes. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: BlockchainFee
columns.comment: Network fee paid in the crypto's native units. Recorded after on-chain confirmation. Used for cost analysis, customer billing, and financial reconciliation. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: CryptoId
columns.comment: The cryptocurrency sent. FK to Wallet.CryptoTypes.CryptoID. Combined with WalletId for per-wallet per-crypto transaction history queries. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactions)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing event identifier. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionStatuses)

columns.name: SentTransactionId
columns.comment: The sent transaction this status event belongs to. FK to Wallet.SentTransactions.Id. Multiple rows per transaction. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionStatuses)

columns.name: StatusId
columns.comment: Blockchain confirmation status: 0=Pending, 1=Confirmed, 2=Verified, 3=Error, 4=Timeout, 5=PermanentError, 6=WavedError. See Transaction Status. FK to Dictionary.TransactionStatus. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionStatuses)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp of this status transition. Used for confirmation time calculations. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.SentTransactionStatuses)

columns.name: gcid
columns.comment: Group Customer ID - cross-product identity key linking the same person across eToro products/entities. NULL for older accounts predating GCID introduction.

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing PK. FK target for Wallet.TravelRuleSends. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: Customer wallet this address belongs to. FK to Wallet.WalletPool.WalletId. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: ToAddress
columns.comment: The whitelisted external blockchain address. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: TravelRuleAddressTypeId
columns.comment: Address type: 1=Private (self-hosted), 2=Hosted (VASP). See Travel Rule Address Type. FK to Dictionary.TravelRuleAddressType. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: SelfAccount
columns.comment: Whether the beneficiary is the same person as the sender: 1=self-transfer, 0=third-party transfer. Affects compliance requirements. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: HostingCompany
columns.comment: Name of the VASP hosting the destination address (from Wallet.HostingCompanies list). NULL for private/self-hosted addresses. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: Name
columns.comment: Beneficiary's full name. MASKED for PII protection. NULL for self-transfers. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: CountryAlpha3Code
columns.comment: Beneficiary's country (ISO 3166 alpha-3). MASKED. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: State
columns.comment: Beneficiary's state/province. MASKED. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: City
columns.comment: Beneficiary's city. MASKED. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: Address
columns.comment: Beneficiary's street address. MASKED. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: Zipcode
columns.comment: Beneficiary's postal code. MASKED. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: Created
columns.comment: Timestamp when this address was whitelisted. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing primary key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleSends)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: Source wallet that performed the send. FK to Wallet.WalletPool.WalletId. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleSends)

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.comment: Links to the parent send request. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleSends)

columns.name: TravelRuleAddressId
columns.comment: The whitelisted address used for this send. FK to Wallet.TravelRuleAddresses.Id. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleSends)

columns.name: Created
columns.comment: Timestamp of this send-address linkage. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.TravelRuleSends)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Balance snapshot surrogate key. From Wallet.WalletBalances.Id. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: WalletAddressesId
columns.comment: The specific WalletAddresses record for this balance's blockchain address. Resolved by JOIN WalletAddresses ON Address = CustomerWalletsView.Address. From Wallet.WalletAddresses.Id. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: DateFrom
columns.comment: Start of balance snapshot validity window. From Wallet.WalletBalances.DateFrom. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: DateTo
columns.comment: End of balance snapshot validity window. 3000-01-01 = current balance. From Wallet.WalletBalances.DateTo. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: Balance
columns.comment: Confirmed crypto balance in native units. From Wallet.WalletBalances.Balance. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: CryptoId
columns.comment: The cryptocurrency. From Wallet.WalletBalances.CryptoId. FK to Wallet.CryptoTypes.CryptoID. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.vw_WalletBalanaces)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing surrogate primary key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: The parent wallet this address belongs to. FK to Wallet.WalletPool.WalletId. Multiple addresses can share the same WalletId (Bitcoin UTXO wallets). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: Address
columns.comment: The raw blockchain address string as provided by the wallet provider. May include protocol prefixes or query parameters. Unique constraint enforced. NULL only for wallets with deferred address generation. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: IsMain
columns.comment: Whether this is the wallet's primary address. 1=main address (used for receiving), NULL/0=secondary address. Most wallets have exactly one main address. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: BlockchainProviderWalletId
columns.comment: Provider-side wallet identifier (BitGo or CUG wallet ID). Used for API calls to the custody provider. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: CustomerWalletStatusId
columns.comment: Activation state: 0=Pending (awaiting blockchain confirmation), 1=Active (ready for transactions). See Customer Wallet Status. FK to Dictionary.CustomerWalletStatus. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp when this address record was created. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: BalanceAccountID
columns.comment: External balance account identifier used by the provider for balance tracking. NULL for wallets without provider-side balance accounts. Unique constraint (filtered, non-NULL only). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: NormalizedAddress
columns.comment: Computed PERSISTED column that strips protocol prefixes (before ':') and query parameters (after '?') from the Address. Enables consistent address matching regardless of formatting. Indexed for lookup performance. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAddresses)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing surrogate primary key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAssets)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: The wallet this asset belongs to. FK to Wallet.Wallets.WalletId. Combined with CryptoId for unique constraint. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAssets)

columns.name: CryptoId
columns.comment: The cryptocurrency asset. FK to Wallet.CryptoTypes.CryptoID. A wallet can hold multiple cryptos - each is a separate WalletAssets row. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAssets)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: When this asset was first added to the wallet. Represents the moment the user first acquired this crypto. Used for "portfolio age" analytics. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAssets)

columns.name: IsShown
columns.comment: Whether this asset is visible in the wallet UI. 1=shown (default), 0=hidden. Allows users or the system to hide zero-balance or deprecated assets without deleting the record. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletAssets)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing surrogate key for row identification. Not used as FK by other tables - the composite PK is the business key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: The wallet this balance belongs to. Part of composite clustered PK. Implicit reference to Wallet.WalletPool.WalletId. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: CryptoId
columns.comment: The cryptocurrency this balance measures. FK to Wallet.CryptoTypes.CryptoID. Part of composite clustered PK. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: DateFrom
columns.comment: Start of this balance snapshot's validity window. Set to the time the balance was confirmed by the provider. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: DateTo
columns.comment: End of this balance snapshot's validity window. 3000-01-01 = current/open balance. Updated to the next snapshot's DateFrom when a new balance is recorded. Part of composite clustered PK. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: Balance
columns.comment: The confirmed crypto balance in native units (e.g., BTC, ETH). NULL is possible but rare - indicates the balance could not be determined. Uses high-precision decimal for sub-unit accuracy. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp when this balance record was created/updated in the database. May differ from DateFrom if there was processing delay. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletBalances)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing surrogate key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: Internal wallet identifier (GUID). The primary business key used across the wallet system. Unique constraint. FK target for Wallet.WalletAddresses, Wallet.ReceivedTransactions, and Wallet.AmlValidations. Also referenced by Wallet.Wallets (logical link, not FK). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: BlockchainCryptoId
columns.comment: The blockchain this pool wallet was created for. FK to Wallet.BlockchainCryptos.Id. Determines which blockchain network the PublicAddress belongs to. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: ProviderWalletId
columns.comment: Wallet identifier assigned by the external custody provider (BitGo or CUG). Used for all API interactions with the provider. Format varies by provider. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: PublicAddress
columns.comment: Blockchain address associated with this wallet. Users send crypto to this address. NULL during initial creation before address generation completes. Format depends on blockchain (e.g., bc1... for BTC, 0x... for ETH). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: Created
columns.comment: Timestamp when this pool wallet was created. Used for pool age monitoring and FIFO assignment ordering. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: WalletProviderId
columns.comment: Custody provider that holds the keys: 1=BitGo, 2=CUG, 3=None. See Wallet Provider. FK to Dictionary.WalletProvider. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPool)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing event identifier. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: WalletPoolId
columns.comment: The pool wallet this status event belongs to. FK to Wallet.WalletPool.Id. Multiple status rows per wallet (event-sourced). (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: WalletPoolStatusId
columns.comment: The lifecycle status: 1=Pending, 2=Verified, 3=Failed, 4=FundingInitiated, 5=FundingSent, 6=FundingVerified, 7=FundingFailed, 10=Timeout, 11=VerifiedForAssign. See Wallet Pool Status. FK to Dictionary.WalletPoolStatuses. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp of this status transition. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: PromotionTagId
columns.comment: Links to a promotional campaign if this wallet is part of a promotion. NULL for standard wallets. FK to Wallet.PromotionTags.Id. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: CorrelationId
columns.comment: Links this status event to the parent request that triggered the transition. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: Processed
columns.comment: Whether downstream systems have consumed this status event: 0=pending processing, 1=processed. Used by the assignment system to find wallets ready for customer assignment. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: CryptoId
columns.comment: The cryptocurrency this pool wallet is for. FK to Wallet.CryptoTypes.CryptoID. Denormalized from WalletPool for efficient status-based filtering. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.WalletPoolStatuses)

columns.name: Id
columns.comment: Auto-incrementing surrogate key. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: WalletId
columns.comment: Internal wallet identifier (GUID). Unique constraint. The universal business key used across the entire system. FK target for Wallet.SentTransactions, Wallet.Conversions, Wallet.Payments, Wallet.Redemptions, and Wallet.WalletAssets. Matches the WalletId in Wallet.WalletPool from which this wallet was assigned. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: Gcid
columns.comment: Global Customer ID of the wallet owner. For system wallets (types 1-4, 6-7), this is a system/service account ID. For customer wallets (type 5), this is the real user. Indexed for per-customer lookups. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: BlockchainCryptoId
columns.comment: The blockchain network this wallet operates on. FK to Wallet.BlockchainCryptos.Id. Combined with Gcid and WalletTypeId for unique customer wallet constraint. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: WalletTypeId
columns.comment: Operational purpose: 1=Redeem, 2=Conversion, 3=Funding, 4=Payment, 5=Customer (99.99%), 6=C2F, 7=StakingRefund. See Wallet Type. FK to Dictionary.WalletTypes. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: IsActive
columns.comment: Whether this wallet is currently operational. 1=active, 0=deactivated (funds locked, no new transactions). Set to 0 by Wallet.DeactivateWallet. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: Occurred
columns.comment: Timestamp when this wallet was created/assigned to the customer. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: BeginDate
columns.comment: System-versioned temporal column (ROW START). Tracks when this version of the row became current. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: EndDate
columns.comment: System-versioned temporal column (ROW END). Default 9999-12-31 for current rows. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)

columns.name: IsActivated
columns.comment: Whether the wallet has completed blockchain activation. 1=fully activated, 0=pending activation (awaiting on-chain confirmation). Most wallets are immediately activated. (Tier 1 - upstream wiki, WalletDB.Wallet.Wallets)
